title: Report Contiguous Dates
description: Find contiguous date ranges in Failed and Succeeded tables. Report period_state, start_date, end_date.
constraints: fail_date is the primary key of Failed
success_date is the primary key of Succeeded
Only dates in year 2019 should be considered
No date appears in both tables
Contiguous ranges within the same state are merged; a switch in state starts a new range
key_concept: Gaps-and-islands with two period types. UNION ALL both, apply ROW_NUMBER grouping per state.

hints.L1: UNION ALL Failed and Succeeded with state labels. Apply gaps-and-islands per state.
hints.L2: Island key: DATE - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY state ORDER BY date). Same key = same island.
hints.L3: WITH all_dates AS (SELECT fail_date dt,'failed' state FROM Failed WHERE YEAR(fail_date)=2019 UNION ALL SELECT success_date,'succeeded' FROM Succeeded WHERE YEAR(success_date)=2019), grp AS (SELECT dt, state, SUBDATE(dt, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY state ORDER BY dt)) grp_key FROM all_dates) SELECT state period_state, MIN(dt) start_date, MAX(dt) end_date FROM grp GROUP BY state, grp_key ORDER BY start_date
